commit.message:
chore: Update `ShowMessageBox` to `ShowMessageBoxAsync` and upgrade package versions (#760)

* Add `GetCallStackAsync` method to `IActivityExecutionService` and its implementation in `RemoteActivityExecutionService`.

* Add call stack viewer to `WorkflowInstanceViewer` with activity selection support in left panel tabs

* Add `SelectActivityByIdAsync` method to `WorkflowInstanceWorkspace` and update null initialization for fields

* Refactor `Journal.razor`: simplify structure by removing unused `MudTabs`, streamline nested components, and enhance styling logic.

* Remove unused `MudDynamicTabs` reference and obsolete namespace import from `WorkflowInstanceWorkspace.razor`.

* Add call stack visualization for activity executions

Introduced components and models to display a call stack for activity executions in the Workflow Instance Viewer. This includes UI elements for call stack rendering, error handling, and data integration with activity execution records.

* Add `ExecutionSelected` callback to `ActivityExecutionsTab` and invoke it on activity selection changes

* Add support for `SelectedActivityExecutionId` and `ActivityExecutionSelected` callbacks in `WorkflowInstanceViewer`

* Remove support for activity execution selection callbacks.

This refactor eliminates unused `ActivityExecutionSelected` callbacks and associated parameters across multiple components. It simplifies the codebase by removing redundant logic and dependencies related to activity execution selection while preserving core functionality.

* Add pinning support to call stack and improve activity selection logic

Introduced the ability to pin the call stack, allowing it to remain fixed during navigation. Enhanced activity selection by optimizing early exits, preventing redundant calls, and adding logic for bidirectional highlighting within the current call stack.

* Enhance call stack handling with `ActivityExecutionSelected` logic and streamline activity execution integration

* Refactor activity execution details and improve UX.

Introduced a persistent drawer for execution details and refactored related components for better modularity and readability. Updated the data model and logic to streamline activity execution handling and display refreshed details dynamically.

* Set default pin state and optimize activity execution handling

Set `_isPinned` to true to ensure consistent pin behavior. Added checks and updates for activity execution details, improving null handling and refresh logic. Increased maximum receive message size to 5MB for hub connections.

* Refactor `Journal.razor`: remove unused `Color` property and streamline icon styling logic.

* Update src/modules/Elsa.Studio.Workflows/Shared/Components/DiagramDesignerWrapper.razor.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unused `Models` import and redundant icon style calculation from `ActivityCallStack.razor`.

* Make WorkflowInstanceDetails and WorkflowInstanceDesigner readonly for immutability in WorkflowInstanceWorkspace.razor.cs

* Remove readonly keyword from WorkflowInstanceDetails and WorkflowInstanceDesigner in WorkflowInstanceWorkspace.razor.cs for mutability purposes.

* Refactor `ActivityCallStack` and `WorkflowInstanceViewer` for streamlined UX

Removed pinning feature and unused variables in `ActivityCallStack`. Refined drawer opening logic in `WorkflowInstanceViewer` for smoother interaction. Enhanced styling and streamlined event callbacks for better performance.

* Enhance journal selection logic and refactor workflow log record selection handling

Improved journal entry selection checks, added `_isSelectingFromJournal` flag to prevent unwanted activity selections, and refactored workflow execution log record selection logic with try-finally for consistent state management.

* Refactor `WorkflowInstanceViewer` to improve programmatic selection handling

Replaced `_isSelectingFromCallStack` and `_isSelectingFromJournal` flags with `_programmaticallySelectedNodeId` to streamline the handling of node selections and prevent unintended state changes.

* Enhance `ActivityCallStack` with selected entry tracking and virtualization

Added `_selectedEntry` to track selected call stack entries. Implemented virtualization in `ActivityCallStack.razor` to improve performance for long lists. Streamlined entry selection and clearing logic.

* Implement activity name retrieval in `WorkflowInstanceViewer`

- Added functions to fetch custom and default activity names by ID.
- Updated `Journal`, `ActivityCallStack`, and workflow viewer components to use these functions for consistent activity name presentation.
- Enhanced UX by prioritizing designer activity name, with fallbacks to custom names and activity types.

* Update `ShowMessageBox` to `ShowMessageBoxAsync` and upgrade package versions

- Refactor calls to use `ShowMessageBoxAsync` for consistency and async operations.
- Upgrade various package versions to improve dependency management and get latest features.

* Upgrade `CodeBeam.MudBlazor.Extensions` to `9.0.0-rc.1` and optimize `ActivityPicker.razor`

- Update package version for `CodeBeam.MudBlazor.Extensions` to leverage new features.
- Refactor `ActivityPicker.razor` for improved localization handling and consolidation of logic.
- Simplify `ActivityTreeItem` constructor by integrating `ChildrenList`.

commit.message:
EXP-1271-1280: feature exhaustion confirmed, strategic pivot

Final prediction pipeline benchmark (EXP-1280):
  Naive=-9.96, Base=0.457, Transfer=0.492, Full=0.496 (11/11 wins)

Confirmed findings:
- EXP-1271: Uniform averaging > Ridge stacking (+0.006, R²=0.496)
- EXP-1276: Error-aware PIs achieve 80.6% coverage (target 80%)
- EXP-1277: Cross-patient generalization gap only 3.5%
- EXP-1274: Cross-horizon averaging HURTS short horizons (-0.038)

Feature engineering exhausted (all ≤ 0.001):
- Velocity/acceleration, variability, interactions, temporal
  augmentation, recent emphasis, adaptive transfer all ~zero

Strategic assessment: 280 experiments confirm glucose forecasting
at 60-min has converged (R²≈0.50 CV). The remaining 0.35 gap to
noise ceiling requires unobserved data (meals, activity). The
physics pipeline's value is in therapy detection/recommendation,
not point prediction.

* fix(core): add flush_spans() to drain BatchSpanProcessor before sandbox suspend (#46181)

BatchSpanProcessor exports spans on a background timer (default 5s).
In hosted sandbox environments the platform may suspend the process
immediately after an HTTP response is sent, before the timer fires.
This causes short-lived spans (e.g. LangGraph per-node invoke_agent
spans) to be lost.

Add flush_spans() to the core public API and call it from
trace_stream's finally block so the streaming path also flushes.

Verified: same agent produces 19-31 spans with flush vs 11 without.

fix: Docker build — copy full pnpm workspace deps instead of individual node_modules

The COPY instruction doesn't support shell operators (2>/dev/null || true),
so copying non-existent packages/shared/node_modules caused a hard failure.

Fix: copy entire dep tree from deps stage with 'COPY --from=deps /app ./'
which preserves pnpm's hoisted workspace structure correctly.

Validate version on release (#22)

Fixes https://github.com/github/spark/issues/2259

Our typical release process for this component is just to set a tagged
release and let that go. This has the downside that our binary also
thinks it knows its version over in cmd/version.go.

Rather than do something elaborate when we're not releasing this often,
this PR simply adds a guardrail that will fail the release if the
versions don't align. In that case, the move is to remove the partial
release/tag, commit the updated version, and then release.

This should be sufficient for the moment, and if we get working on this
more often to where it's an annoyance, we can put a more thorough system
in place.

refactor: simplify fact-check to 3 layers (regex+LLM+test)

commit.message:
Integrate CWE domain taxonomies into bootstrap composition flow (#232)

* Integrate CWE domain taxonomies into bootstrap composition flow

Update bootstrap.md guidelines to ask for the audit domain when
assembling security audit and code review prompts (investigate-security,
review-code, review-cpp-code, exhaustive-bug-hunt). The composition
engine now:

- Presents the 13 CWE domain options when a security/review template
  is selected
- Infers the domain from context when possible and asks for confirmation
- Adds the corresponding cwe-<domain> taxonomy to the assembled prompt's
  Classification Taxonomy section alongside template-declared taxonomies
- Allows the user to skip CWE scoping (existing taxonomies still apply)

This is bootstrap-driven (no template contract changes) — the
composition engine dynamically adds the taxonomy at assembly time,
consistent with how PromptKit already handles optional components.

Addresses the template integration task from Issue #228.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add review-cpp-code to CWE taxonomy applicable_to lists

All 13 CWE taxonomy files and the ingestion script now include
review-cpp-code in their applicable_to metadata, matching the
bootstrap guideline that triggers CWE domain selection for
review-cpp-code templates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Scope review-cpp-code applicable_to to C/C++ domains only

Only kernel-mode-c-cpp, native-user-mode-c-cpp, and firmware-embedded
taxonomies should list review-cpp-code — other domains like web-js-ts,
managed-dotnet, crypto-protocols are not C/C++ review targets.

Updated ingestion script to conditionally emit review-cpp-code based
on domain membership in the cpp_domains set.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove applicable_to from CWE taxonomies

The applicable_to field is unused metadata — nothing in bootstrap,
manifest, or tooling reads it. CWE taxonomy selection is driven by
domain (via bootstrap asking the user), not by template name.
Maintaining applicable_to per-template-per-domain creates busywork
and inconsistency bugs with no consumer.

Removed from all 13 CWE taxonomy files and the ingestion script.

fix(maglev): pass closure context (RSI) to Maglev entry point

nodes (previously only LoadNamedGeneric was seeded), enabling inline Smi
arithmetic for promoted loop variables loaded from globals.

commit.message:
fix(seo): add expired data for Artisa Group job to fix canonical→listing

The job 'segretaria-assistente-progetto-artisa-group-lugano' was removed
from active data but never archived to expired, causing its soft-landing
page to have minimal content. Google's canonicalization then chose the
listing page instead of the self-referencing canonical.

This adds proper expired entries with translated titles, descriptions,
and salary data so the build plugin generates enriched soft-landing pages
that Google will respect as canonical.

Root cause: the Artisa crawler removed the job from its slice but the
daily cleanup-jobs.mjs had not yet run to archive it. The compat-paths
pipeline still generated a page, but without expired metadata the content
was too thin for Google to treat as a distinct page.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(eoc-crawler): prevent slug churn from location instability

EOC jobs accumulated 10+ previousSlugs each (958 total), bloating the
build with thousands of redirect pages. Root causes:

1. Location instability: extractEocLocation() produces different cities
   across crawl runs (Bellinzona→Novaggio→Lugano) depending on which
   hospital name appears first in the description. The base crawler's
   merge step treats location changes as genuine slug changes.

2. No cap on previousSlugs: every slug change accumulated indefinitely.

Fixes:
- Add stabilizeEocSlugsInMemory(): compares post-merge jobs with
  pre-crawl slice data by vacancy ID. If the title is unchanged, any
  slug change is location-driven and gets reverted.
- Add previousSlugs capping (MAX_PREVIOUS_SLUGS=5) in
  postProcessEocJobsInMemory(): deduplicates against current slugs,
  then keeps only the 5 most recent entries.
- Cap existing data: 958→660 previousSlugs (31% reduction).

Tests: 12 tests pass (5 new covering stabilization + capping).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  so 'import openchimera' resolved to an empty namespace package and
  'from openchimera import api_server' raised ImportError)

  RequestValidationFailure from core.api_server

  CLI entry-point importable as 'from openchimera import cli; cli.main()'
- Update pyproject.toml packages.find to include 'openchimera*' so the
  new package is discovered and installed by pip/setuptools
